ksa_text: Knowledge of data and AI strategy development approaches, including strategy formulation methods, alignment with enterprise business strategy, competitive positioning through data capabilities, and strategy communication frameworks.

dcwf.top_element_label: Use knowledge of business processes to create or recommend AI solutions.

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: Although Leadership & Strategy is an edge domain for the Director of Data Products, this knowledge KSA presents sufficient signal (composite score 0.498) and conceptual alignment to include at the director level.

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No internal inconsistency identified. Domain scope (Leadership & Strategy), seniority fit, and signal level are internally consistent with inclusion.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Pass 1 signal is moderate (composite 0.498). External reviewers may question whether this specific knowledge KSA is sufficiently distinctive for the Director of Data Products versus adjacent roles.
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: McKinsey, Gartner, and Harvard Business Review consistently identify leadership and strategy skills as core differentiators for C-suite and VP data leaders.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Upheld: moderate framework signal is consistent with Leadership & Strategy appearing in job postings and role definition literature for Director of Data Products.

decision_rationale: Edge-domain Leadership & Strategy knowledge KSA cleared adversarial review with score 0.498. Included based on sufficient framework alignment and role relevance for the Director of Data Products.
